title: Authenticate multi-hop DAO origins
description: Non-Storing DAO forwarding preserves the origin IPv6 source while each relay replaces the link-layer signature. Hop authentication therefore cannot prove DAO origin at the root; an authenticated relay can forge another source/target and Path Sequence. Design and implement cryptographically preserved end-to-end DAO provenance, bound to RPLInstanceID, DODAGID, DAOSequence, ordered Target/Transit options, and origin IPv6 identity, without breaking hop forwarding.
design: Implement the approved terminal provisional DAO Origin Signature option using existing Schnorr48 and pinned Announce identities. The option is unique and final, carries a persistent 64-bit origin sequence plus 48-byte signature, and signs a domain-separated SHA-512 transcript binding origin IPv6, effective DODAGID, origin sequence, and exact unsigned DAO bytes. Relays preserve it unchanged. Roots verify key/IID, signature, and persistent sequence before parsing authorization-sensitive semantics or mutating replay/routes. Exact static prefix delegations are handled by .44.9.2 after provenance.
acceptance_criteria: A documented wire format and trust model identify the origin key; root verifies provenance before DAO replay/path/route mutation; relays can forward without resigning origin content; replay, target substitution, option reordering, withdrawal forgery, and unknown-key tests fail closed; C/Python/Rust protocol/spec/vector implications are tracked; three security reviews pass.
notes: Completed on integration/rust-rpl-complete. Reconciled prefix/path-snapshot model and added production RplStack ownership for radio, RPL, Announce trust, scope-bound DTX2/DRX2/DAD1 persistence, explicit bounded DAO admission, transactional multi-hop Announce relay, DIO/DIS/Trickle control, preferred-parent DAO forwarding, strict IPv6/ICMPv6 and L2 destination validation, durable replay ordering, prefix-egress repair, and exact failure/restart behavior. External 50-vector node test remains intact. Three final independent release reviews found no P0-P2 issues. Validation: Rust lichen-link 29+2+1, lichen-ipv6 13, lichen-rpl 125+2+5, lichen-node 185+2+docs pass; scoped strict Clippy passes except pre-existing scheduler test helper blocks all-target node Clippy; Python DAO vectors 54 pass; independent C/Monocypher oracle 50 pass; Zephyr native_sim/native/64 link_crypto 10/10 pass with explicit ZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES. Broader Zephyr tree: 40 configurations pass, 2 fail, 5 error from pre-existing unrelated app/transport/CoAP/L2 issues. Follow-ups filed: 2auf.67 no_std build and 2auf.68 invalid signed link vector.
close_reason: Authenticated multi-hop DAO origins are fully integrated, reviewed, and validated across Rust, Python/C vectors, and relevant ARM64 Zephyr crypto tests.
